title: Getting Started with stigmaR
text: stigmaR gives researchers pre-computed, state-level structural stigma scores derived Project Implicit IAT data. package’s three functions merge scores existing dataframe matching state abbreviation year. scores cover 50 US states + DC 2016 onward.

title: Getting Started with stigmaR
code: # Install from GitHub remotes::install_github("stigmaRverse/stigmaR") library(stigmaR)

title: Getting Started with stigmaR
text: dataframe needs one column two-letter state abbreviations (e.g., "IL", "CA"). Everything else — participants, outcomes, covariates, etc. package ships small test dataset (data/test_data.xlsx) state column person ID. ’ll use throughout examples:
code: library(readxl) my_data <- read_excel("data/test_data.xlsx")[ , c("state", "person")] head(my_data) #>   state  person #> 1    TX       1 #> 2    CA       1 #> 3    IL       1 #> 4    AL       1 #> 5    GA       1 #> 6    TN       1

title: Getting Started with stigmaR
text: stigmaR() merges one pre-averaged composite indices. combination year × index becomes new column named YYYY_indexname. adds four columns: 2019_iat_sex_implicit, 2020_iat_sex_implicit, 2019_iat_sex_explicit_pol, 2020_iat_sex_explicit_pol. function prints coverage table can see many states matched data column. Available indices (see also names(stigmaR::composite)): "iat_sex_implicit" — IAT D-score (implicit pro-straight bias; higher = stigma) "iat_sex_explicit_therm" — Mean gay men + lesbian women feeling thermometers (reversed) "iat_sex_explicit_pol" — Mean five policy opposition items "iat_sex_explicit" — Omnibus mean across explicit items
code: my_data <- stigmaR(   df    = my_data,   state = "state",   index = c("iat_sex_implicit", "iat_sex_explicit_pol"),   year  = c("2019", "2020") )

title: Getting Started with stigmaR
text: item_stigmaR() works identically stigmaR() merges raw individual items instead composites. Use want inspect analyze items separately combining . adds 2020_iat_sex_imp_d, 2020_iat_sex_exp_therm_gm, 2020_iat_sex_exp_pol_marr. single call capped 100 new columns (length(item) × length(year)) prevent accidental blowups — split larger requests multiple calls. available items (see also names(stigmaR::items)): Respondent counts item stored parallel iat_sex_n_* columns items dataset (e.g., iat_sex_n_imp_d) want apply sample-size thresholds.
code: my_data <- item_stigmaR(   df    = my_data,   state = "state",   item  = c("iat_sex_imp_d", "iat_sex_exp_therm_gm", "iat_sex_exp_pol_marr"),   year  = "2020" )

title: Getting Started with stigmaR
text: cust_stigmaR() lets define sum-score composite(s) individual items. Scores summed row-wise within year; items mixed across years within one composite. Pass list cust_index, one name per group var_name: default (na_rm = FALSE), composite NA state-year component item missing. Set na_rm = TRUE sum whatever available instead:
code: my_data <- cust_stigmaR(   df         = my_data,   state      = "state",   year       = c("2019", "2020"),   cust_index = c("iat_sex_imp_d", "iat_sex_exp_therm_gm"),   var_name   = "implicit_plus_therm" ) # Adds: 2019_implicit_plus_therm, 2020_implicit_plus_therm my_data <- cust_stigmaR(   df         = my_data,   state      = "state",   year       = "2020",   cust_index = list(     c("iat_sex_imp_d", "iat_sex_exp_att"),     c("iat_sex_exp_pol_marr", "iat_sex_exp_pol_legal",       "iat_sex_exp_pol_adopt", "iat_sex_exp_pol_serv",       "iat_sex_exp_pol_trans")   ),   var_name = c("implicit_explicit_sum", "policy_sum") ) # Adds: 2020_implicit_explicit_sum, 2020_policy_sum my_data <- cust_stigmaR(   df         = my_data,   state      = "state",   year       = "2020",   cust_index = c("iat_sex_imp_d", "iat_sex_exp_att"),   var_name   = "my_sum",   na_rm      = TRUE   # sum non-missing items; NA only if ALL items are missing )

title: Getting Started with stigmaR
code: my_data <- cust_stigmaR(   df         = my_data,   state      = "state",   year       = c("2019", "2020"),   cust_index = c("iat_sex_imp_d", "iat_sex_exp_therm_gm"),   var_name   = "implicit_plus_therm" ) # Adds: 2019_implicit_plus_therm, 2020_implicit_plus_therm

title: Getting Started with stigmaR
text: Pass list cust_index, one name per group var_name:
code: my_data <- cust_stigmaR(   df         = my_data,   state      = "state",   year       = "2020",   cust_index = list(     c("iat_sex_imp_d", "iat_sex_exp_att"),     c("iat_sex_exp_pol_marr", "iat_sex_exp_pol_legal",       "iat_sex_exp_pol_adopt", "iat_sex_exp_pol_serv",       "iat_sex_exp_pol_trans")   ),   var_name = c("implicit_explicit_sum", "policy_sum") ) # Adds: 2020_implicit_explicit_sum, 2020_policy_sum

title: Getting Started with stigmaR
text: default (na_rm = FALSE), composite NA state-year component item missing. Set na_rm = TRUE sum whatever available instead:
code: my_data <- cust_stigmaR(   df         = my_data,   state      = "state",   year       = "2020",   cust_index = c("iat_sex_imp_d", "iat_sex_exp_att"),   var_name   = "my_sum",   na_rm      = TRUE   # sum non-missing items; NA only if ALL items are missing )

title: Getting Started with stigmaR
text: three functions use naming convention: YYYY_indexname (YYYY_itemname / YYYY_varname). makes easy reference specific year’s score keeping multiple years wide dataframe.
code: # Reference specific year columns directly my_data$`2020_iat_sex_implicit` my_data[["2020_iat_sex_explicit_pol"]]

title: Getting Started with stigmaR
text: using stigmaR, please cite: Kim, S. & Todd, N. R. (2027). stigmaR: Enhancing Accessibility, Reproducibility, Transparency Structural Stigma Research. Greenwald, . G., Nosek, B. ., & Banaji, M. R. (2003). Understanding using Implicit Association Test. Journal Personality Social Psychology, 85(2), 197–216. https://doi.org/10.1037/0022-3514.85.2.197
code: citation("stigmaR")

title: Measuring and calculating structural stigma
text: goal stigmaR measure calculate structural stigma. questions, ideas, need help? Join discussion board →

title: Measuring and calculating structural stigma
text: can install development version stigmaR GitHub :
code: install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("stigmaRverse/stigmaR")

title: Composite Structural Stigma Indices — composite
text: State- year-level composite (pre-averaged) structural stigma indices derived Project Implicit IAT data, covering 50 US states DC 2016 onward. Used internally stigmaR().

title: Composite Structural Stigma Indices — composite
code: composite

title: Composite Structural Stigma Indices — composite
text: data frame 510 rows (51 states + DC x 10 years) 6 columns: state Two-letter US state code (50 states + DC ). year 4-digit integer year. iat_sex_implicit IAT D-score (implicit pro-straight bias; higher = stigma). iat_sex_explicit_therm Mean gay men + lesbian women thermometers (reversed: higher = stigma). iat_sex_explicit_pol Mean 5 policy opposition items. iat_sex_explicit Omnibus mean across explicit items (thermometer + policy).

title: Composite Structural Stigma Indices — composite
text: https://github.com/stigmaRverse/stigmaRdata

what: Build a Custom Sum-Score Composite Index — cust_stigmaR
title: Build a Custom Sum-Score Composite Index — cust_stigmaR
text: Lets combine individual items bundled items dataset sum-score composite(s), merged data state year. composite row-wise sum items choose, computed within single year (mix items different years one composite). merge individual items without combining , see item_stigmaR().

title: Build a Custom Sum-Score Composite Index — cust_stigmaR
code: cust_stigmaR(df, state, year, cust_index, var_name, na_rm = FALSE)

title: Build a Custom Sum-Score Composite Index — cust_stigmaR
text: df data frame containing data. state Character string. Name column df containing two-letter state abbreviations (e.g., "IL", "CA"). year Character vector. Years merge (e.g., c("2016", "2017")). custom index computed separately requested year added column named YYYY_varname. cust_index character vector item names sum single custom index, list character vectors build multiple custom indices . Valid item names determined automatically columns bundled items dataset (every iat_sex_* mean column, excluding iat_sex_n_* respondent-count columns). See names(stigmaR::items) full, current list. var_name Character vector names new column(s), one per group cust_index (.e., length(var_name) must equal length(cust_index), 1 cust_index single vector). na_rm Logical. FALSE (default), composite NA given state-year component items NA state-year. TRUE, composite sum whatever component items non-missing (NA missing).

title: Build a Custom Sum-Score Composite Index — cust_stigmaR
text: input data frame new columns named YYYY_varname containing summed custom index requested year var_name.

title: Build a Custom Sum-Score Composite Index — cust_stigmaR
text: Kim, S. & Todd, N. R. (2027). stigmaR: Enhancing Accessibility, Reproducibility, Transparency Structural Stigma Research.

title: Build a Custom Sum-Score Composite Index — cust_stigmaR
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # One custom index new_df <- cust_stigmaR(   df         = my_data,   state      = "state_abbrev",   year       = c("2016", "2017"),   cust_index = c("iat_sex_imp_d", "iat_sex_exp_therm_gm"),   var_name   = "my_custom_index" )  # Multiple custom indices at once new_df <- cust_stigmaR(   df         = my_data,   state      = "state_abbrev",   year       = "2016",   cust_index = list(     c("iat_sex_imp_d", "iat_sex_exp_therm_gm"),     c("iat_sex_exp_pol_marr", "iat_sex_exp_pol_legal", "iat_sex_exp_pol_adopt")   ),   var_name   = c("implicit_plus_therm", "policy_sum") ) } # }

what: Calculate State-Level Individual Item Scores — item_stigmaR
title: Calculate State-Level Individual Item Scores — item_stigmaR
text: Merges state- year-level individual item scores - building blocks behind composite indices stigmaR() - dataset. Use want full flexibility work individual items (e.g., build compare composites). create single summed composite column directly, see cust_stigmaR().

title: Calculate State-Level Individual Item Scores — item_stigmaR
code: item_stigmaR(df, state, item, year)

title: Calculate State-Level Individual Item Scores — item_stigmaR
text: df data frame containing data. state Character string. Name column df containing two-letter state abbreviations (e.g., "IL", "CA"). item Character vector. One individual items merge . set valid items determined automatically columns bundled items dataset (every iat_sex_* mean column, excluding iat_sex_n_* respondent-count columns). See names(stigmaR::items) full, current list. year Character vector. Years merge (e.g., c("2016", "2017")). year x item combination becomes one new column named YYYY_itemname (example).

title: Calculate State-Level Individual Item Scores — item_stigmaR
text: input data frame new columns named YYYY_itemname containing matched state-level item scores requested year item.

title: Calculate State-Level Individual Item Scores — item_stigmaR
text: keep merges manageable, single call limited 100 new columns (.e., length(item) * length(year)). need , split request multiple calls.

title: Calculate State-Level Individual Item Scores — item_stigmaR
text: Kim, S. & Todd, N. R. (2027). stigmaR: Enhancing Accessibility, Reproducibility, Transparency Structural Stigma Research.

title: Calculate State-Level Individual Item Scores — item_stigmaR
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ new_df <- item_stigmaR(   df    = my_data,   state = "state_abbrev",   item  = c("iat_sex_imp_d", "iat_sex_exp_therm_gm"),   year  = c("2016", "2017") ) } # }

what: Individual Item Structural Stigma Scores — items
title: Individual Item Structural Stigma Scores — items
text: State- year-level individual item means respondent counts underlying composite indices composite. Used internally item_stigmaR() cust_stigmaR().

title: Individual Item Structural Stigma Scores — items
code: items

title: Individual Item Structural Stigma Scores — items
text: data frame 510 rows (51 states + DC x 10 years) 20 columns: state, year, 9 item-mean columns (iat_sex_*), 9 matching respondent-count columns (iat_sex_n_*): state Two-letter US state code (50 states + DC ). year 4-digit integer year. iat_sex_imp_d IAT D-score (d_biep.straight_good_all). iat_sex_exp_att Explicit attitude item (att_7). iat_sex_exp_therm_gm Gay men thermometer (reversed). iat_sex_exp_therm_gw Lesbian women thermometer (reversed). iat_sex_exp_pol_marr Marriage rights opposition. iat_sex_exp_pol_legal Relations legality opposition. iat_sex_exp_pol_adopt Adoption opposition. iat_sex_exp_pol_serv Service refusal support. iat_sex_exp_pol_trans Transgender bathroom opposition. iat_sex_n_imp_d Respondent count iat_sex_imp_d. iat_sex_n_exp_att Respondent count iat_sex_exp_att. iat_sex_n_exp_therm_gm Respondent count iat_sex_exp_therm_gm. iat_sex_n_exp_therm_gw Respondent count iat_sex_exp_therm_gw. iat_sex_n_exp_pol_marr Respondent count iat_sex_exp_pol_marr. iat_sex_n_exp_pol_legal Respondent count iat_sex_exp_pol_legal. iat_sex_n_exp_pol_adopt Respondent count iat_sex_exp_pol_adopt. iat_sex_n_exp_pol_serv Respondent count iat_sex_exp_pol_serv. iat_sex_n_exp_pol_trans Respondent count iat_sex_exp_pol_trans.

title: Individual Item Structural Stigma Scores — items
text: https://github.com/stigmaRverse/stigmaRdata

what: stigmaR: Measuring and calculating structural stigma — stigmaR-package
title: stigmaR: Measuring and calculating structural stigma — stigmaR-package
text: Provides researchers pre-computed, state-level structural stigma scores derived publicly available data. Users can merge stigma indices—including implicit bias, explicit attitudes, policies, belief items—data frames using single function call.

title: stigmaR: Measuring and calculating structural stigma — stigmaR-package
text: Maintainer: Seungju Kim follhim@gmail.com Authors: Seungju Kim follhim@gmail.com

title: Calculate Structural Stigma Scores — stigmaR
text: Merges state- year-level structural indices derived publicly available data dataset. can find available indices stigmaRdata.

title: Calculate Structural Stigma Scores — stigmaR
code: stigmaR(df, state, index, year)

title: Calculate Structural Stigma Scores — stigmaR
text: df data frame containing data. state Character string. Name column df containing two-letter state abbreviations (e.g., "IL", "CA"). index Character vector. One composite indices merge . set valid indices determined automatically columns bundled composite dataset (every column except state year). Examples include: "iat_sex_implicit": IAT D-score (implicit pro-straight bias; higher = stigma) "iat_sex_explicit_pol": Average policy opposition items (marriage rights, relations legality, adoption, service refusal, transgender bathroom) See names(stigmaR::composite) full, current list. year Character vector. Years merge (e.g., c("2016", "2017")). year x index combination becomes one new column named YYYY_iat_sex_implicit (exeample).

title: Calculate Structural Stigma Scores — stigmaR
text: input data frame new columns named YYYY_iat_sex_implicit containing matched state-level stigma scores requested year index.

title: Calculate Structural Stigma Scores — stigmaR
text: Kim, S. & Todd, N. R. (2027). stigmaR: Enhancing Accessibility, Reproducibility, Transparency Structural Stigma Research.

title: Calculate Structural Stigma Scores — stigmaR
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ new_df <- stigmaR(   df    = my_data,   state = "state_abbrev",   index = c("iat_sex_implicit", "iat_sex_explicit_pol"),   year  = c("2016", "2017") ) } # }

title: Version 0.1.0 (2026-06-15)
text: Added IAT-sexuality composite indices stigmaR(). Added item_stigmaR() merging individual item scores. Added cust_stigmaR() building custom sum-score composites individual items.
